lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਕਿਛੁ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਕਾ ਸੋ ਸੇਵਕ ਕਾ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਹੀ ਸੰਗਿ ਜਾਹਰੁ ਜੀਉ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jo kichh tthaakur kaa so sevak kaa sevak tthaakur hee sang jaahar jeeo |3|
lines.english: That which belongs to the Lord and Master, belongs to His servant. The servant becomes distinguished in association with his Lord and Master. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਠਾਕੁਰ ਕੇ ਸੇਵਕ ਹਰਿ ਰੰਗ ਮਾਣਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: tthaakur ke sevak har rang maaneh |
lines.english: The servant of the Lord and Master enjoys the Love and Affection of the Lord.
Is not called to answer for his account any longer.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਸੇਵਕ ਕੈ ਨਾਨਕ ਕੁਰਬਾਣੀ ਸੋ ਗਹਿਰ ਗਭੀਰਾ ਗਉਹਰੁ ਜੀਉ ॥੪॥੧੮॥੨੫॥
lines.translit: tis sevak kai naanak kurabaanee so gahir gabheeraa gauhar jeeo |4|18|25|
lines.english: Nanak is a sacrifice to that servant. He is the pearl of the deep and unfathomable Ocean of God. ||4||18||25||
He, whom the Lord and Master dresses in the robes of honor,
Maajh, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭ ਕਿਛੁ ਘਰ ਮਹਿ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਨਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: sabh kichh ghar meh baahar naahee |
lines.english: Everything is within the home of the self; there is nothing beyond.
One who searches outside is deluded by doubt.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦੀ ਜਿਨੀ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਪਾਇਆ ਸੋ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਸੁਹੇਲਾ ਜੀਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: gur parasaadee jinee antar paaeaa so antar baahar suhelaa jeeo |1|
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, one who has found the Lord within is happy, inwardly and outwardly. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਦ ਬਿਨੋਦ ਕਰੇ ਦਿਨ ਰਾਤੀ ਸਦਾ ਸਦਾ ਹਰਿ ਕੇਲਾ ਜੀਉ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: anad binod kare din raatee sadaa sadaa har kelaa jeeo |2|
lines.english: It enjoys bliss and ecstasy day and night, and plays with the Lord forever and ever. ||2||
Slowly, gently, drop by drop, the stream of nectar trickles down within.
The mind drinks it in, hearing and reflecting on the Word of the Shabad.
by the Grace of the Holy Saint, the dried-up branches have blossomed forth again in their greenery.
I have now been united with the Lord after having been separated and cut off from Him for so many lifetimes;

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਮਤਿ ਪਾਏ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਿਆਏ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹੋਏ ਮੇਲਾ ਜੀਉ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: sumat paae naam dhiaae guramukh hoe melaa jeeo |3|
lines.english: I have obtained this sublime understanding, and I meditate on the Naam; as Gurmukh, I have met the Lord. ||3||
As the waves of water merge again with the water,
so does my light merge again into the Light.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਭ੍ਰਮ ਕਟੇ ਕਿਵਾੜਾ ਬਹੁੜਿ ਨ ਹੋਈਐ ਜਉਲਾ ਜੀਉ ॥੪॥੧੯॥੨੬॥
lines.translit: kahu naanak bhram katte kivaarraa bahurr na hoeeai jaulaa jeeo |4|19|26|
lines.english: Says Nanak, the veil of illusion has been cut away, and I shall not go out wandering any more. ||4||19||26||
I am a sacrifice to those whose tongues speak of You.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਾਰਿ ਵਾਰਿ ਜਾਈ ਤਿਸੁ ਵਿਟਹੁ ਜੋ ਮਨਿ ਤਨਿ ਤੁਧੁ ਆਰਾਧੇ ਜੀਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: vaar vaar jaaee tis vittahu jo man tan tudh aaraadhe jeeo |1|
lines.english: Again and again, I am a sacrifice to those who meditate on You with mind and body. ||1||
I am a sacrifice to those who have heard of You.
Maajh, Fifth Mehl:
With my eyes, I long to behold those kind people.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਸੁ ਚਰਣ ਪਖਾਲੀ ਜੋ ਤੇਰੈ ਮਾਰਗਿ ਚਾਲੈ ॥
lines.translit: tis charan pakhaalee jo terai maarag chaalai |
lines.english: I wash the feet of those who walk upon Your Path.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੁ ਦੇਵਾ ਤਿਸੁ ਅਪੁਨੇ ਸਾਜਨ ਜਿਨਿ ਗੁਰ ਮਿਲਿ ਸੋ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਲਾਧੇ ਜੀਉ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: man devaa tis apune saajan jin gur mil so prabh laadhe jeeo |2|
lines.english: I offer my mind to those friends, who have met the Guru and found God. ||2||
Very fortunate are those who know You.
In the midst of all, they remain detached and balanced in Nirvaanaa.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਧ ਕੈ ਸੰਗਿ ਉਨਿ ਭਉਜਲੁ ਤਰਿਆ ਸਗਲ ਦੂਤ ਉਨਿ ਸਾਧੇ ਜੀਉ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: saadh kai sang un bhaujal tariaa sagal doot un saadhe jeeo |3|
lines.english: In the Saadh Sangat, the Company of the Holy, they cross over the terrifying world-ocean, and conquer all their evil passions. ||3||
I have renounced my pride in my own strength, and the darkness of emotional attachment.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨ ਕੀ ਸਰਣਿ ਪਰਿਆ ਮਨੁ ਮੇਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: tin kee saran pariaa man meraa |
lines.english: My mind has entered their Sanctuary.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੁ ਦਾਨੁ ਦੀਜੈ ਨਾਨਕ ਕਉ ਤਿਸੁ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਅਗਮ ਅਗਾਧੇ ਜੀਉ ॥੪॥੨੦॥੨੭॥
lines.translit: naam daan deejai naanak kau tis prabh agam agaadhe jeeo |4|20|27|
lines.english: Please bless Nanak with the Gift of the Naam, the Name of the Inaccessible and Unfathomable God. ||4||20||27||
Maajh, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂੰ ਜਲਨਿਧਿ ਤੂੰ ਫੇਨੁ ਬੁਦਬੁਦਾ ਤੁਧੁ ਬਿਨੁ ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਭਾਲੀਐ ਜੀਉ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: toon jalanidh toon fen budabudaa tudh bin avar na bhaaleeai jeeo |1|
lines.english: You are the Ocean of Water, and You are the foam and the bubbles on its surface. I cannot see any other except You, Lord. ||1||
From the very small and subtle, You have become huge and manifest.
You are the tree; Your branches have blossomed forth.
You are the knot, and You are the primary bead of the maalaa.
You are the thread, and You are also the beads.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਦਿ ਮਧਿ ਅੰਤਿ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਸੋਈ ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਕੋਇ ਦਿਖਾਲੀਐ ਜੀਉ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: aad madh ant prabh soee avar na koe dikhaaleeai jeeo |2|
lines.english: In the beginning, in the middle and in the end, there is God. I cannot see any other except You, Lord. ||2||
You are detached in Nirvaanaa, and You are the Enjoyer, imbued with love.
You transcend all qualities, and You possess the supreme qualities. You are the Giver of peace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਪਣੇ ਕਰਤਬ ਆਪੇ ਜਾਣਹਿ ਆਪੇ ਤੁਧੁ ਸਮਾਲੀਐ ਜੀਉ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: apane karatab aape jaaneh aape tudh samaaleeai jeeo |3|
lines.english: You Yourself know Your Own Ways; You dwell upon Yourself. ||3||
You are the Master, and then again, You are the servant.
O God, You Yourself are the Manifest and the Unmanifest.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਦਾਸੁ ਸਦਾ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਵੈ ਇਕ ਭੋਰੀ ਨਦਰਿ ਨਿਹਾਲੀਐ ਜੀਉ ॥੪॥੨੧॥੨੮॥
lines.translit: naanak daas sadaa gun gaavai ik bhoree nadar nihaaleeai jeeo |4|21|28|
lines.english: Slave Nanak sings Your Glorious Praises forever. Please, just for a moment, bless him with Your Glance of Grace. ||4||21||28||
